gate: G3b — on-box Qwen3-TTS optimized (A2/A3, plan 2026-07-22-onbox-audio-perf-queue-whisper.md workstream A)
box: MS02 (RTX 2000 Ada 16GB, torch 2.13.0+cu130, qwen-tts-venv, fork kunzite-app/Qwen3-TTS-streaming 80d21b6e)
method: Same 1835-char reply POSTed to Orchestrator /tts/batch (provider=qwen, voice=qwen:Vivian) end-to-end (chunker -> llama-swap -> member -> stitcher). Wall from HTTP roundtrip; audio seconds parsed from the returned WAV. >=3 runs per config (sampling variance is +/-14% audio-length for identical text at the production do_sample/T=0.9 defaults).
result: PASS — long-reply >=4x target met: 112.7s -> 26.8s mean (4.2x, outlier included), 20.5s median (5.5x)

before_sequential_600char.description: Pre-A3 shipping path: 600-char chunks, one /v1/audio/speech HTTP call per chunk, sequential under _qwen_batch_lock (4 chunks)

native_batch_600char_b4.description: A3 native batch, chunking left at 600 chars -> ONE member request, one padded generate at b=4

native_batch_300char_b8_SHIPPED.description: A3 + qwen chunk default 300 chars when native batch on -> 8 chunks, one padded generate at b=8 (batch wall = LONGEST sample, so narrower chunks widen the batch AND shorten the critical path)

cold_run.note: includes llama-swap member spawn + 1.7B variant load

warm_runs.note: long-sample tail: one chunk sampled ~50s extra audio and held the whole batch (audit-predicted; T=0.9 sampling variance, not a regression)

single_chunk_590char.description: b=1 through the same pipeline after A3 — unchanged as expected (batching amortizes the per-frame nested code_predictor.generate overhead; b=1 has nothing to amortize)

compile_a2.status: implemented + measured, shipped DEFAULT OFF (env QWEN_TTS_OPTIMIZE=1 to enable; tiered degrade talker+predictor -> predictor-only -> eager; warmup synth at variant load)
compile_a2.invocation: enable_streaming_optimizations(use_compile=True, use_cuda_graphs=False, use_fast_codebook=False, compile_codebook_predictor=True, compile_talker=True) — cuda_graphs dropped (fork skips it under reduce-overhead: no-op), fast_codebook off (fork: 'needs debugging, currently slower')

side_instance_measurements.b2_first_note: ~21s recompile embedded (new batch shape); torch automatic dynamic shapes kick in here
side_instance_measurements.b4_first_note: NO recompile spike — dynamic kernel covers b>=3 after the b=2 recompile

compile_a2.decision: DEFAULT OFF, measured: compiled dynamic-shape kernels make the BATCHED hot path SLOWER than eager (b=4 RTF 0.25-0.27 compiled vs 0.222 eager; before-change b=4 end-to-end 0.213-0.245), while the 1.54x b=1 win costs ~33s+21s warmup per residency — re-paid after every ttl:600 idle unload AND every audio<->retrieval exclusive-group swap (every embed/auto-mint). Verified honestly per plan A2: the fork's compile DOES apply to the non-streaming path (talker+predictor forward is shared), the ~6x advertised figure is streaming-decode-only, and batch gains from compile are NEGATIVE. No stability issues on torch 2.13.0+cu130 / sm_89 (no tier degrade triggered).

quality_check: 3 known sentences batch-synthesized (b=3) -> transcribed back via on-box Systran/faster-whisper-large-v3: all three intact, correct order, no padding artifacts or truncation (one STT-side mishearing 'Batch'->'Scratch').
regression_found_and_fixed: First 300-char deploy collapsed back to sequential speed (RTF 0.72) within three long replies: the batch VRAM guard read torch.cuda.mem_get_info device-free, which counts torch's own caching-allocator reserved pool (~13GB after a few padded batches) as USED -> cap degraded to b=1. Fixed by probing batch_free_vram_mb = device-free + max(0, reserved - allocated); regression test added (test_batch_guard_prefers_reusable_pool_probe).

shipped_config.QWEN_TTS_NATIVE_BATCH: default ON (per-chunk sequential path kept as fallback + for old members)
shipped_config.QWEN_TTS_OPTIMIZE: default OFF (see compile_a2.decision — measured net-negative for the batched hot path; code + warmup + tiered degrade ship env-gated)
shipped_config.qwen_chunk_default: 300 chars when native batch enabled, 600 for the sequential fallback
